name: spritefusion-pixel-snapper
description: A tool to snap pixels to a perfect grid. Designed to fix messy and inconsistent pixel art generated by AI.
readme_summary: Sprite Fusion Pixel Snapper is a Rust-based tool designed to clean up messy pixel art, particularly that generated by AI, by snapping pixels to a perfect grid and quantizing colors. It ensures consistency in pixel size and position, making art suitable for game development and scalable textures.
key_features: Snaps pixels to a perfect grid
Ensures consistent grid resolution
Quantizes colors to a strict palette
Preserves details like dithering
CLI for batch processing and optional arguments (k-colors, pixel-size)
Web (WASM) module for integration into web projects
primary_use_case: Cleaning and standardizing AI-generated or procedurally created pixel art to fit a strict grid and color palette, making it suitable for game development and scalable assets.
cover_image_prompt: A meticulous digital artisan, akin to a clockmaker, carefully adjusting tiny, chaotic gears (representing messy AI-generated pixels) on a workbench. With a specialized magnifying loupe and a set of fine tools, the artisan is snapping each gear into a perfectly aligned, uniform grid. Subtle glowing lines indicate the grid structure, and a small color palette floats nearby, showing the quantized colors being applied. The background is a clean, modern workshop with faint digital schematics visible. The overall mood is one of precision and order being restored to digital chaos. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, bright aesthetic, emphasizing the transformation from disorder to order.

name: DnsServer
readme_summary: Technitium DNS Server is an open-source, self-hostable DNS server that enhances privacy and security by blocking ads and malware at the DNS level. It offers advanced features like DNS-over-TLS, DNS-over-HTTPS, and DNS-over-QUIC, and provides network insights through logs and statistics.
key_features: Self-hostable DNS server for privacy and security
Blocks ads and malware at DNS level
Supports DNS-over-TLS, DNS-over-HTTPS, and DNS-over-QUIC
User-friendly web console
Works out-of-the-box with minimal configuration
Cross-platform compatibility (Windows, Linux, macOS, Raspberry Pi)
Docker image available
Built-in Clustering feature
Single Sign-On (SSO) with OpenID Connect (OIDC)
High performance with async IO
Authoritative and recursive DNS server support
DNS logs and stats for network insights
primary_use_case: Self-hosting a private and secure DNS server to block ads, malware, and gain network insights.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old owl perched atop a network router, wearing a monocle and meticulously sorting incoming data streams. The owl's talons are sorting glowing streams of 'good' data (represented by green light) into a secure vault, while 'bad' data (represented by red, jagged streams) is being deflected away by a shimmering shield. Small, subtle screens around the owl display DNS queries and blocklist statistics. The background is a softly lit, organized digital landscape. The overall mood is one of vigilant protection and efficient management. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style with a clean, modern aesthetic, emphasizing clarity and security. The repository name 'Technitium DNS Server' is subtly etched on the vault.

name: facex
description: Full face stack that runs entirely in the browser. Detection, 576-point 3D mesh, recognition, anti-spoof, smile — all WebAssembly, zero server. Apache 2.0.
readme_summary: FaceX is a comprehensive face processing pipeline that runs entirely within the browser using WebAssembly. It offers face detection, 576-point 3D mesh generation, recognition, and anti-spoofing capabilities without requiring any server-side processing or dependencies.
key_features: Runs entirely in the browser via WebAssembly
Includes face detection, 576-point 3D mesh, recognition, and anti-spoofing
Zero server-side dependencies
Trained from scratch with custom models
Encrypted weights (AES-256-GCM) for browser security
Low latency (~3 ms per face)
Part of a larger pure-C stack for embedded systems
primary_use_case: Enabling real-time, on-device face analysis and recognition directly in web applications without server infrastructure.
cover_image_prompt: A digital alchemist in a minimalist, browser-like laboratory, holding a glowing prism that refracts a live camera feed into multiple, distinct streams of information: one stream forms a detailed 3D facial mesh, another shows a recognition profile, and a third detects a 'spoof' alert. The alchemist is surrounded by floating, abstract representations of code and data, with the 'FaceX' logo subtly etched onto the prism. The overall mood is one of efficient, self-contained digital magic. The style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a bright, futuristic color palette and subtle glowing effects to emphasize the browser-based, zero-server nature of the technology.

name: Continuum
description: A persistent, multi-agent Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. Continuum gives AI coding agents — Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Gemini CLI — a shared, live view of a codebase and a memory that survives across agents and sessions.
readme_summary: Continuum is a persistent, multi-agent Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to provide AI coding agents with a shared, live view of a codebase and a long-term memory. It allows multiple agents to collaborate on a project, maintaining context and state across sessions and agents.
key_features: Live code graph with tree-sitter parsing for multiple languages
Hybrid code search combining lexical (BM25) and semantic (model2vec) search
Code navigation features (definitions, callers, dependency graphs)
Cross-agent memory for architectural decisions and action history
Daemon and thin adapter architecture for stateful persistence
primary_use_case: To enable persistent, collaborative AI coding agent workflows by providing a shared, live codebase context and inter-agent memory.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing a vast, interconnected library where each book represents a piece of code. The librarian uses a magical magnifying glass that not only reveals the content of the books but also shows how they relate to each other, forming glowing threads of connection. Different AI agents, depicted as helpful sprites, are seen reading from the books and adding new notes to a shared, ethereal ledger. The library itself is a blend of ancient architecture and subtle digital interfaces, with the repository name 'Continuum' subtly etched on a central pillar. The scene is illuminated by a soft, intellectual glow, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clarity and interconnectedness.

name: portabase
description: Portabase - Database backup & restore tool for PostgreSQL, MySQL, MsSQL, MariaDB, Firebird SQL, SQLite, MongoDB, Redis and Valkey
readme_summary: Portabase is a self-hosted, open-source tool designed to simplify database backup and restoration across a wide range of popular database systems. It leverages secure agents for operations and offers multiple installation methods including CLI, Docker, and Kubernetes.
key_features: Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MsSQL, MariaDB, Firebird SQL, SQLite, MongoDB, Redis, and Valkey
Automated CLI, Docker, Docker Compose, and Kubernetes (Helm) installation
Integrates with Portabase agents for secure operations
Self-hosted and open-source
Provides a dashboard for managing backups
primary_use_case: Simplifying and automating the backup and restoration of multiple database types for self-hosted environments.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing and safeguarding a vast collection of ancient, glowing tomes, each representing a different type of database (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, etc.). The librarian is carefully placing backup scrolls into a secure vault, while simultaneously retrieving other scrolls to restore damaged books. Subtle digital particles and glowing UI elements around the vault hint at the automated, software-driven nature of the process. The Portabase logo is subtly etched onto the vault door. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting library setting with soft, focused lighting, using a 3D isometric illustration style with clear, distinct elements.

name: telegram-autopilot
description: Personal AI bot that responds to Telegram messages on your behalf using LLM
readme_summary: Telegram-autopilot is a personal AI assistant that integrates with your Telegram profile to automatically respond to messages on your behalf. It leverages LLMs and Telegram's official Business Mode to handle routine communication, allowing users to maintain their personal style and context.
key_features: Responds in user's style via system prompt
One-button On/Off control
Two types of notes (contact and AI-generated)
Smart context awareness (sender, history, contact info)
Tone detection and adaptation
Admin dashboard for stats, chat history, prompt editing, model selection
Automatic model fallback
Compatibility with any OpenAI-compatible API
primary_use_case: Automating responses to Telegram messages using an AI assistant, allowing users to delegate routine communication.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot assistant wearing a headset, sitting at a desk with a laptop. The laptop screen displays a chat interface where the robot is typing a response. Incoming messages are represented by glowing envelopes flowing into the robot's side, and outgoing responses are represented by outgoing digital streams. Subtle Telegram icons are visible on the desk. The background is a clean, modern office space with soft lighting. The overall mood is helpful and efficient. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant colors and clear, friendly character design, emphasizing seamless communication and AI assistance.
